resources.string:
Command: %1$s
Risk: %2$s
The whitelist is not restored, but destructive commands require manual confirmation.

%1$s
Safety gate: type exactly `%2$s` to continue.
Choose the app interface language. System default follows Android language settings.

File created: %1$s
Includes logs, USB/ADB/Fastboot details, app information, and explicitly selected diagnostic files. Attach it to a bug report when needed.
ERROR: log file has not been created yet

File: %1$s
The log is saved automatically. You can copy the path or send the log as a .txt file through the system menu.
✅ Battery optimization is already disabled for the app
For long flashing, it is better to disable battery restrictions for this app. This lowers the risk that Android stops the process during file transfer.
ERROR: could not open battery settings: %1$s
[Terminal] Full ADB/Fastboot mode is active. Whitelist removed.
log folder is not created yet
Flashing %1$s to partition %2$s. Do not disconnect the cable.
Sending %1$s. Do not disconnect the cable.
Operation is running. Do not disconnect the cable.
Notification shown during flashing or ADB Sideload

Current status:
All files access: %1$s Notifications: %2$s Battery optimization exception: %3$s Overlay protection: %4$s
Used permissions: • All files access — reads .img/.zip/.sha256/.md5 from /sdcard/Download/NekoFlash. • Notifications — shows a foreground operation notification. • Wake lock — keeps CPU awake only during flashing or sideload and is released after the operation. • Hide overlay windows — on Android 12+ blocks other apps from drawing over this app during sensitive actions.
Not requested: • Draw over other apps / SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW. • Internet access. • Contacts, SMS, microphone, camera, phone, or location.
not required on this Android version
not supported on this Android version
ERROR: could not open app settings: %1$s
✅ Overlay protection enabled: other apps cannot cover this app window.
⚠️ Overlay protection could not be enabled: %1$s
Overlay protection is not available before Android 12.
Interface v2.1: full ADB/Fastboot terminal enabled without strict whitelist. SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW is not requested.
Queue is empty. Choose a partition below and select an .img file.

The app will flash these images in order:

%1$s

Checks: Fastboot connection, getvar cache, max-download-size, and sidecar SHA-256/MD5 files. The strict partition list is removed; verify model and slot before continuing.

The following flash queue will be executed:
%1$s
This writes images to bootloader partitions. Review model, slot, and file names before confirming.
Warning: %1$s looks like %2$s, but you selected partition %3$s.
Сначала отметьте согласие с рисками прошивки.
Если MIUI не открыла батарею: Настройки → Приложения → NekoFlash → Батарея → Без ограничений.
Не удалось сохранить подтверждение онбординга. Повторите вход.
Уведомления были отклонены несколько раз. Откройте настройки NekoFlash и разрешите их вручную; вход в приложение не заблокирован.
Раздел «%1$s» критичен. Ошибка может привести к bootloop. Подтвердите ещё раз.
The inventory has not been collected yet. Connect a device in Fastboot and tap Refresh.
No partitions match the selected filter.
Read-only: this list does not authorize or start flashing.
Choose one image and one confirmed partition. No ROM batch flashing and no automatic retry.
Expert targets are hidden. dtbo, vbmeta, vendor_kernel_boot and manual partition are unavailable.
Expert targets are visible for this screen session. Every target still requires device evidence and confirmation.
Enter the exact partition name twice. Defined targets and full-ROM/radio/bootloader partitions are rejected.
The two partition names do not match.
This partition is not allowed in manual Quick Flash.
Enable Expert Mode before selecting this target.
Run the read-only Fastboot data refresh first. Quick Flash will not guess a partition or slot.

File: %1$s Size: %2$s SHA-256: %3$s
Only partitions confirmed by the read-only topology builder are shown.
No confirmed concrete partition exists for this target. Refresh Fastboot data and check the device topology.

Device: %1$s Image: %2$s Size: %3$s SHA-256: %4$s Target: %5$s Concrete partition: %6$s Slot: %7$s Evidence: %8$s Command: %9$s Plan fingerprint: %10$s
%11$s
The USB/Fastboot session changed after the plan was created. Build a new plan.
Expert Mode was disabled after the plan was created. Select the target again.
Quick Flash Expert Mode enabled for the current screen session.
Quick Flash Expert Mode disabled; expert targets hidden.
Legacy multi-flash queue is hidden in Recovery-first Quick Flash.
Recovery → Apply update → Apply from ADB
Before transfer, the app checks SHA-256/MD5 if a .sha256 or .md5 file is next to the archive.
adb shell / adb shell getprop / fastboot getvar all
RUNNING — keep screen on and do not disconnect USB/OTG
COMPLETED — review the log/report before rebooting
FAILED — stop and inspect the report before retrying
WARNING — review the last event before continuing
Step queue: no active plan yet
NOVICE: guided operations are available; manual terminal input is hidden; high-risk actions stay blocked.
High-risk actions: unlocked for this session

This enables destructive actions such as clean all, writes to super/userdata/metadata, and dangerous Fastboot terminal commands. Use only after checking the ROM, product, bootloader state, and recovery path.
Action blocked: %1$s. Current profile: %2$s. Switch to Pro and unlock High Risk to continue.

💡 This looks like PC batch/shell script syntax ("%1$s"), not a fastboot/adb command — the device has no such wire command. Open the .bat/.sh file and run its fastboot/adb commands one at a time, skipping control lines like echo/if/for/set/pause/labels.
Delete imported files from the working folder

Delete all files in: %1$s
This cannot be undone.

NekoFlash v%1$s
Android USB-host flashing tool (ADB/Fastboot). Flash without a PC — phone flashes phone.
Use at your own risk. Flashing can brick a device.
🔁 A/B-capable device detected (slot-count=2). Choose Active, A, B, or Both; NekoFlash will verify the concrete partition target before writing.
📱 Single-slot device confirmed (slot-count=1, no A/B). The both-slots option is hidden — just use the regular flash button.
🧭 Slot-count does not prove A/B. NekoFlash will verify has-slot and partition-size before writing. If a single-slot base partition is confirmed, it will flash the base target without A/B selection.
⚠️ Do not disconnect the cable or minimize the app
Interrupting a flash mid-write can leave the partition in a broken state. Cancel anyway?
🔌 OTG: not supported by this phone
🔌 OTG: no devices · check cable or enable OTG in settings
Could not open settings automatically. Grant "All files access" to NekoFlash manually in Settings → Apps.

This interface has a vendor-specific bulk IN/OUT pair, but its descriptors do not confirm Android Fastboot. Continue only if the target device is visibly in Fastboot mode.
%s
PRO: full terminal and advanced tools are available. High Risk stays locked until separately confirmed.
PRO + HIGH RISK: full terminal and high-risk actions are enabled. Typed confirmations are still required.
High-risk actions are available only in Pro profile. Switch to Pro first, then unlock High Risk.
This action requires Pro profile with the necessary safety level enabled.
Safe download-only transport test. No partition is selected and no flash, boot, stage, or update-super command is sent.

Product: %1$s File: %2$s Exact size: %3$s Transport: %4$s
The selected file is first copied into private internal staging, fsynced, and SHA-256 verified. Only the verified staged copy is sent to Fastboot\'s volatile download buffer. No partition is selected and no flash, boot, stage, or update-super command is sent. Qualification is bound to this exact SHA-256, transport, and current USB session. Native USBFS is diagnostic-only and does not authorize real flashing.

Product: %1$s Size: %2$s Transport: %3$s
No partition is selected. No write command will be sent. The test stops after the final response to the DATA phase. Native USBFS is diagnostic-only.
No Fastboot device is connected. Connect a device and try again.

Product: %1$s File: %2$s Size: %3$s Transport: %4$s Folder: %5$s
The app will create a deterministic .img in shared storage, copy it to private internal storage, fsync it, verify source and staged SHA-256, delete the shared source, and send only the private staged copy to Fastboot\'s volatile download buffer. No flash, boot, stage, or update-super command is sent.
This validates internal Fastboot DATA staging without writing any partition.

Product: %1$s Size: %2$s
The app will test all Native USBFS profiles sequentially in one fresh Fastboot session. It will not send flash, boot, stage, or update-super. If any profile fails, the session is closed and the automatic test stops.

File: %1$s Size: %2$s Transport: %3$s
The app will create private diagnostic prefix files from the selected .img and test the first 1/2/3/4/8/16 MB via download-only. This helps identify which file region breaks DATA. No write command is sent.
Login was stopped: navigation to an untrusted host (%1$s) was blocked.
The Xiaomi Account page could not be loaded.
Login was stopped because the Xiaomi Account TLS certificate could not be verified.
The secure login component stopped unexpectedly. Please try again.
Xiaomi completed the sign-in, but the required unlockApi session cookies were not returned. Try again or sign out of Xiaomi Account in the browser first.
Login was cancelled by the user.
Could not open the folder. The path was copied.
